markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Generate bridging edges between fragmented subgraphs only when evidence supports the bridge and hub suppression is considered. This MT belongs to MemoryGraphAndClaims. Implement the Handshake-native memory system translated from research into product contracts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Paper-derived memory architecture must be expressed as Handshake-native MemoryOntology, MemoryFact, MemoryPassage, ClaimLifecycle, ConflictResolution, and EvidenceSpan contracts.
Probationary claims cannot become stable retrieval facts without evidence, conflict checks, and EventLedger promotion.
scope.expected_behavior: BridgeEdgeGenerator leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: BridgeEdgeGenerator is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 71548244; MemoryGraphAndClaims group: 21 memory-graph tests pass on real PostgreSQL (ontology/facts/conflict/bridge/projection/api/fixtures); migrations 0240-0243 with down+rollback test; facts back knowledge_claims (reuse lifecycle+conflict authority), projections marked authority_class=projection.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-124 BridgeEdgeGenerator requires generating proposed relates_to bridge edges across fragmented subgraphs only when endpoints share evidence, sit in different edge components, and neither is a hub, with a full audit decision log on PostgreSQL. The commit (71548244) implements exactly this with real union-find connectivity, evidence-gated bridging, hub suppression, an audited decision table (migration 0243), and routes edge creation through the committed knowledge_edges substrate. Proof runs against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL via the isolated-schema migration-chain fixture, no SQLite/mocks.
findings: bridge.rs:46-99 implements a real union-find (disjoint-set with path compression) over non-retired workspace edges; connectivity is computed per pass via list_active_edge_endpoints - genuine algorithm, not a stub.

bridge.rs:108-200 generate_bridge_edges enforces all three MT gates: suppressed_connected when already in same component, suppressed_hub when either endpoint degree >= hub_degree_threshold, otherwise creates a proposed relates_to edge backed by the shared span and unions the pair. Every considered pair is recorded.

bridge.rs:172-189 creates the bridge via db.upsert_knowledge_edge then set_knowledge_edge_lifecycle(Proposed) - it does NOT bypass the committed edge substrate, so the spec-mandated span trigger, deterministic relationship_id, and lifecycle all hold. Bridge is born proposed (suggestion), never auto-accepted.

migrations/0243_knowledge_memory_bridge_edges.sql: real audit table knowledge_memory_bridge_decisions with FK refs to workspaces/knowledge_entities/knowledge_spans/knowledge_edges, decision CHECK enum, and shape CHECKs (chk_kmbd_bridged_shape requires edge+span for 'bridged'; chk_kmbd_suppressed_no_edge forbids edge for suppressions). Registered in knowledge_schema_registry with authority_class='authority'. down.sql provided.

storage/knowledge_memory.rs:1219-1297,1365+ provide real sqlx SQL: find_entity_cooccurrences (entity-span self-join, ordered distinct pairs), list_active_edge_endpoints, entity_edge_degree (COUNT over non-retired edges), record_bridge_decision (real INSERT). No stubs.

tests/knowledge_memory_bridge_tests.rs: two #[tokio::test] integration tests against real managed PostgreSQL (MemoryFixture::setup -> knowledge_pg isolated schema running sqlx::migrate!('./migrations') full chain incl. 0243). Asserts bridge is RelatesTo+Proposed, carries the shared span as evidence, decision logged, idempotent re-suppression on second pass, and hub endpoint suppressed with no bridge. SKIPs loudly if PG absent - not a mock substitute.

Spec v02.194 section 2.3.13.11 (02-system-architecture.md:2694-2719) mandates KnowledgeEdge MUST carry source span refs, extractor version, lifecycle state, confidence, and stable relationship_id, and that graph-mutation proposals leave receipts - the implementation matches exactly. 'proposed' is a valid lifecycle state per the spec and the KnowledgeEdgeLifecycle enum (knowledge.rs:1074-1097).

No deferred-live escapes: grep of bridge.rs + tests + knowledge_memory.rs for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-'/Unavailable found none; the prior placeholder doc line was replaced with the real implementation.
Self-cert: lifecycle.claimed_by and completed_by are both null (this is a READY_FOR_VALIDATION handoff for external COMPLETED transition), so sub_rule_3 is NA - no malformed self-certification.

Aside (non-blocking, expected paperwork drift): packet/MT cite v02.192 spec anchors and packet spec_bundle points at v02.193; current is v02.194. Same section numbers resolve and content is present - not a code defect.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
